title: artemisia caerulescens subsp. cretacea
intro: Artemisia caerulescens subsp. cretacea, also known as Tibetan wormwood, is a subspecies of the artemisia caerulescens plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Tibetan wormwood include reducing fever, treating gastrointestinal issues, and easing respiratory problems. Tibetan wormwood can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its purported antimalarial properties.
